content.problemStatement:
The country of Chefland has NNN cities, with its capital being at city 111.

Unfortunately, the only source of water in the entire country is the capital, and so water must be sent via pipes to all the other cities.

For each city i>1i \gt 1i>1, there is a unique city pi<ip_i \lt ipi‚Äč<i such that there is a pipe connecting cities iii and pip_ipi‚Äč. Note that this ensures every city is connected to city 111 via a unique sequence of pipes.

Over time, the pipes have degraded and all now leak water.

The pipe connecting cities iii and pip_ipi‚Äč has a leakiness of AiA_iAi‚Äč, which means that every AiA_iAi‚Äč-th unit of water passing through it is lost entirely.

For example, with a leakiness of 444, if 131313 units of water enter the pipe, only 101010 will leave it (the 444-th, 888-th, 121212-th unit will be lost).

On each day iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), the capital (city 111) generates KKK units of water, which are then all sent to city iii via the pipes (other cities lying on the path between 111 and iii don't get to keep any of this water).

Due to the leakiness of the pipes, some of these KKK units of water will be lost as the water passes sequentially through the pipes.

Let RiR_iRi‚Äč denote the amount of water received by city iii.

Each day is completely independent - that is, all the pipes reset at the start of each day, and behave as if no water has passed through them before.

See the sample cases below for more clarity.

You would like to maximize min‚Ā°(R1,R2,‚Ä¶,RN)\min(R_1, R_2, \ldots, R_N)min(R1‚Äč,R2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,RN‚Äč), i.e. maximize the minimum amount of water received by any city.

To achieve this, **at most once**, you can choose an index iii (2‚Č§i‚Č§N2 \le i \le N2‚Č§i‚Č§N), and set Ai:=K+1A_i := K+1Ai‚Äč:=K+1.

Functionally, this ensures that no water leaks through this pipe.

Find the maximum possible value of min‚Ā°(R1,R2,‚Ä¶,RN)\min(R_1, R_2, \ldots, R_N)min(R1‚Äč,R2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,RN‚Äč) after fixing at most one pipe.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚ÄĒ the number of cities and the daily water generated by city 111.
The second line contains N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 space-separated integers p2,p3,‚Ä¶,pNp_2, p_3, \ldots, p_Np2‚Äč,p3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the parents of the non-capital cities.
The third line contains N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 space-separated integers A2,A3,‚Ä¶,ANA_2, A_3, \ldots, A_NA2‚Äč,A3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the leakiness values of the pipes.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible value of min‚Ā°(R1,R2,‚Ä¶,RN)\min(R_1, R_2, \ldots, R_N)min(R1‚Äč,R2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,RN‚Äč) after fixing at most one pipe.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are two cities, with 111 connected to 222 with a pipe of leakiness 888.
We can just fix this pipe, and there are no leaky pipes - so cities 111 and 222 will both receive K=43K = 43K=43 units of water, which is the best we can do.
Test case 222: There are three cities, with cities 222 and 333 both directly connected to city 111 via pipes of leakiness 444 and 777 respectively.
We have two choices:

Fix the pipe between 111 and 222.

City 111 will receive all K=29K = 29K=29 units of water.
City 222 will also receive all K=29K = 29K=29 units of water, since the pipe connecting it to 111 is fixed.
City 333 will receive 252525 units of water - 292929 units will flow from 111 to 333 via a pipe of leakiness 777, so every seventh unit will be lost - meaning units 7,14,21,287, 14, 21, 287,14,21,28 will be lost, for a total of four.


Fix the pipe between cities 111 and 333.

Cities 111 and 333 will now receive K=29K = 29K=29 units of water.
City 222 will receive 29‚ąí7=2229 - 7 = 2229‚ąí7=22 units of water, since 777 units will be lost when 292929 pass through a pipe will leakiness 444.



The first option has the minimum water received at 252525, while the second option has it at 222222; so the first option is better and the answer is 252525.
Test case 333: The optimal solution is to choose i=5i = 5i=5 and fix the pipe between 555 and p5=3p_5 = 3p5‚Äč=3. with K=197K = 197K=197, this results in the following:

Day 111: 197197197 units of water are sent to city 111. No pipes are needed, no water is lost, so R1=197R_1 = 197R1‚Äč=197.
Day 222: 197197197 units of water are sent to city 222, along the path 1‚Üí21\to 21‚Üí2. The only pipe along the way is A2=15A_2 = 15A2‚Äč=15, and 131313 units of water will be lost. So, R2=197‚ąí13=184R_2 = 197 - 13 = 184R2‚Äč=197‚ąí13=184.
Day 333: 197197197 units of water are sent to city 333, along the path 1‚Üí2‚Üí31\to 2\to 31‚Üí2‚Üí3.
The first pipe is A2=15A_2 = 15A2‚Äč=15, so just as before, 131313 units of water will be lost, and 184184184 units will reach city 222.
The second pipe is A3=20A_3 = 20A3‚Äč=20, and with 184184184 units passing through it, 999 units will be lost.
This results in R3=184‚ąí9=175R_3 = 184 - 9 = 175R3‚Äč=184‚ąí9=175.
Day 444: 197197197 units of water are sent to city 444, along the path 1‚Üí41\to 41‚Üí4.
The only pipe is A4=17A_4 = 17A4‚Äč=17, which results in R4=186R_4 = 186R4‚Äč=186.
Day 555: 197197197 units of water are sent to city 555, along the path 1‚Üí2‚Üí3‚Üí51\to 2\to 3\to 51‚Üí2‚Üí3‚Üí5.
As seen earlier, 175175175 units will reach city 333, and then because we fixed the pipe between 333 and 555, all of these will reach city 555, for R5=175R_5 = 175R5‚Äč=175.

This obtains min‚Ā°(R1,R2,R3,R4,R5)=175\min(R_1, R_2, R_3, R_4, R_5) = 175min(R1‚Äč,R2‚Äč,R3‚Äč,R4‚Äč,R5‚Äč)=175, which is optimal here.
Test case 444: No matter which pipe is fixed, some city will receive 000 units of water.
